2026-04-08T10:14:36.7458073Z 
2026-04-08T10:14:40.3925894Z ● dispatch_triage (MCP: safeoutputs)
2026-04-08T10:14:40.3926305Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-04-08T10:14:40.3926466Z 
2026-04-08T10:14:43.6048369Z Filed a GitHub issue titled **"[Management Generator] Docs: Document extension resource detection with specific parent types"** and dispatched triage.
2026-04-08T10:14:43.6049134Z 
2026-04-08T10:14:47.4934604Z **Summary:** The push (PR #57722 by @live1206) fixed a bug in `resource-detection.ts` and related files for detecting extension resources with specific parent types. The existing `OperationContext.md` only covers the generic/wildcard extension resource case and doesn't explain the specific-parent-type scenario. Additionally, no `CHANGELOG.md` exists in the mgmt generator package to track this fix. The issue includes an implementation guide for a coding agent to address both gaps.

2026-04-08T10:14:51.3300528Z   
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3301047Z   Let me look at what the OperationContext.md says about extension resources:
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3301848Z   
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3302145Z   > **Extension Resources**
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3303069Z   > Extension resources have a flexible scope (can extend any resource). Their contextual path starts with a single variable segment:
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3304064Z   > ```
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3304529Z   > /{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3305141Z   > ```
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3305946Z   > The system handles these specially, treating `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource's Id.
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3306849Z   
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3307621Z   This is vague and doesn't mention the concept of extension resources with specific parent types.
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3308273Z

2026-04-08T10:14:51.3318303Z   1. `OperationContext.md` - mentions extension resources but only the generic case with `/{resourceUri}/`
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3319371Z   2. `resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` - migration guide for resolveArmResources API
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3320231Z   3. `emitter.md` - emitter options, no mention of extension resources
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3320762Z   
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3321007Z   
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3321583Z   4. `README.md` - basic usage and emitter options only
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3322039Z   
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3323635Z   The main gaps are that `OperationContext.md` doesn't cover extension resources with specific parent types, there's no developer documentation explaining how the generator handles different extension resource scenarios, and there's no CHANGELOG to track this fix for users.
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3325452Z   
2026-04-08T10:14:51.3326576Z   I should file an issue to document extension resources with specific parent types in `OperationContext.md` - what they are, how they differ from generic ones, and the generator's handling of them.

2026-04-08T10:14:51.5811920Z         "name": "report_incomplete",
2026-04-08T10:14:51.5813772Z         "description": "Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully."
2026-04-08T10:14:51.5813848Z       },
